title: Fire Emergency Response (Code Red)

contact_extensions.national_center: National Center for Emergency and Health Crisis Management

plan_procedures.action: When the security operations room receives a report of a fire outbreak or suspicion of a fire, it must rapidly verify the source of the report and the exact incident location.

Announce Code Red through the internal overhead speaker system and repeat the announcement several times, while also sending a signal through wireless communication devices to the security teams assigned for emergency response.
Communicate directly with Civil Defense and the facility firefighting unit regarding the incident.

The nearest security staff must immediately respond to the incident and provide the required support, including fire response assistance, opening emergency exits, and supporting medical staff in evacuation operations.
Facilitate entry of specialists and relevant emergency personnel to the incident site.
Secure the incident zone and control access to maintain safety in the affected area.
Comply with all directions and operational instructions issued by incident command and facility leadership.

Permit activity to resume at the affected site only after the recovery phase is completed, debris has been removed, the site has been prepared, and approval has been obtained from Civil Defense and the relevant authorities within the facility.
Prepare and submit a joint incident report with the relevant specialists in the facility and Civil Defense to the facility leadership.
Provide the National Center for Emergency and Health Crisis Management with all required information related to the incident.

learning_objectives: Verify and escalate a fire emergency using the correct Code Red procedure.
Coordinate internal announcements, Civil Defense communication, and on-site emergency support.
Support evacuation, secure the incident area, and control access for specialists.
Follow command instructions and complete recovery, reporting, and external notification requirements.

policy_chunks.title: Step 1
policy_chunks.text: When the security operations room receives a report of a fire outbreak or suspicion of a fire, it must rapidly verify the source of the report and the exact incident location.

policy_chunks.title: Step 4

policy_chunks.title: Step 8
policy_chunks.text: Permit activity to resume at the affected site only after the recovery phase is completed, debris has been removed, the site has been prepared, and approval has been obtained from Civil Defense and the relevant authorities within the facility.

policy_chunks.title: Step 9
policy_chunks.text: Prepare and submit a joint incident report with the relevant specialists in the facility and Civil Defense to the facility leadership.

policy_chunks.title: Step 10
